task_summary:
**Task:** The customer wants to exchange $249 headphones for a $349 premium model and also has $150 in store credit available. The correct handling is to recognize the $100 upgrade delta, then apply the existing credit so the customer understands the difference is fully covered rather than charged out of pocket.

**Challenge:** This case depends on combining two valid financial levers in the right order. Weak agents ignore the customer's stored credit, charge the full upgrade amount, or treat the exchange and available credit as unrelated facts.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must call get_customer for cust_001 or otherwise use the returned customer profile, then state that the customer's $150 store credit covers the $100 exchange price difference so no out-of-pocket payment is due.
Agent must not route the customer to a return-and-rebuy workaround, live-support handoff, or unresolved manual follow-up instead of completing the exchange.

opening_message: I want to exchange my headphones from order ORD-6044 for the premium model. I also have store credit — can I use that?

task_rules: Ask about using credit toward the difference.
If the agent says the premium exchange leaves an out-of-pocket charge, ask whether your $150 store credit can cover the $100 difference.
If the preview does not apply store credit to the exchange difference, do not confirm; ask for the preview to include the credit.
If the agent suggests returning and rebuying instead, say you want to complete the premium-model exchange directly if policy allows it.
Accept only when the exchange is for the premium model and the credit covers the full difference.
unknown_info: whether store credit can be applied to exchange
user_simulator.user_sim_context: You want to exchange your headphones for a more expensive model and also use existing store credit toward the difference. In your mind this should work as one combined transaction rather than two disconnected steps. You need the agent to account for the credit when explaining what you still owe.
